name: codex-augment-dispatcher
description: Unified Codex plugin for external CLI adapters that supplement Codex with planning, research, review, frontend, and future capabilities.

interface.shortDescription: Route Codex work through extensible external CLI adapters.
interface.longDescription: Codex Augment Dispatcher is an extensible adapter hub for external CLIs and Codex background thread fanout that fill gaps in Codex workflows while preserving Codex as the local executor. The initial adapters are Claude CLI for numbered task gating and follow-up review, Grok CLI for external research, critique, creative direction, divergence, and Grok-video-only assets, and AGY CLI for frontend implementation under Codex supervision. Future CLI adapters can be added as separate skills, scripts, tests, and dispatch rules without renaming the plugin.
defaultPrompt: Use the dispatch skill first when a task might benefit from an external CLI adapter; the initial adapters are Claude, Grok, and AGY.
Use read-only Codex background threads for research, planning, frontend checks, and review when independent work can shorten the critical path.
Codex owns local execution, verification, commits, and final claims; external CLIs are advisory or delegated helpers with explicit boundaries.
Never pass secrets or unnecessary private context to Grok, Claude, or AGY; no fallback provider is allowed for Grok/video/image-generation paths.
